description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a solid, dark metal head. The handle is made from a light-colored wood with visible grain patterns, providing both strength and a comfortable grip. The axe head is wedge-shaped, with a broad, flat poll on one side and a sharp, beveled cutting edge on the other, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. The transition between the handle and the head is seamless, with the head securely mounted onto the handle, likely using a traditional wedged fitting. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This tool is commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas for cutting wood or similar materials.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark, wedge-shaped metal head, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The tool has a utilitarian appearance with no visible branding or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad face of the metal axe head.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the curvature of the handle and the wedge-shaped geometry of the axe head.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the poll of the axe head and the end grain of the wooden handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the handle's curve and the beveled cutting edge of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, oval cross-section, ergonomic for gripping, no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single wedge-shaped steel blade with a beveled cutting edge and a flat poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the head fitted onto the handle.
